popularStory.headline: Eleanor of Castile (1241-28 November 1290)
popularStory.description:
Eleanor of Castile (1241-28 November 1290) was an English queen, the first wife of Edward I, whom she married as part of a political deal to affirm English sovereignty over Gascony.

The marriage was …

sourceList.title: The Year of Eleanor of Castile's Birth and Her Children By Edward I , by John Parsons in Mediaeval Studies, Volume 46, page 245-265

zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor of Castile Queen of England於1240年1月10日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯時，她的父親Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León38歲，母親Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla19歲。她於1254年10月18日與Edward I King of England在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和11個女兒的父母。她去世於1290年11月28日在英國諾廷加姆爾哈比享年50歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor of Castile Queen of England于1240年1月10日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯时，她的父亲Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León38岁，母亲Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla19岁。她于1254年10月18日与Edward I King of England在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和11个女儿的父母。她去世于1290年11月28日在英国諾廷加姆爾哈比享年50岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Castile
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of   Casteel   1, a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Casteel
meanings: Altered form of mainly northern French Ducastel: topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, from Picard castel, Old French chastel ‘castle’ (see Castel 5, compare 2 below); or a habitational name for someone from any of several places called Le Castel. Compare Castile .
Dutch: from kasteel ‘castle’, hence a topographic name or a metonymic occupational name for someone who lived or worked in a castle.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor of Castile Queen of England was born on 10 January 1240, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, was 38 and her mother, Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla, was 19. She married Edward I King of England on 18 October 1254, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 11 daughters. She died on 28 November 1290, in Harby, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor of Castile Queen of England est née le 10 janvier 1240, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Edward I King of England le 18 octobre 1254, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 11 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 novembre 1290, à Harby, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor of Castile Queen of England am 10. Januar 1240 in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León 38 und ihre Mutter Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla 19. Sie hat am 18. Oktober 1254 in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Edward I King of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 11 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. November 1290 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Harby, Nottinghamshire, England und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor of Castile Queen of England è nata il il 10 gennaio 1240 nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna, sua padre, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Edward I King of England il 18 ottobre 1254, nel Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 11 figlie. Morì il 28 novembre 1290, nel Harby, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor of Castile Queen of Englandが1240年1月10日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスで生まれた時，父Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de Leónは38歳，母Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castillaは19歳であった。1254年10月18日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーでEdward I King of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と11人の娘の親であった。彼女は1290年11月28日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアハービーで50歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León,: 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 버고스 버고스 버고스
ko.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor of Castile Queen of England(이)가 1240년 1월 10일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 버고스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1254년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 Edward I King of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1290년 11월 28일 영국 노틴그함실르 하르비에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor of Castile Queen of England nasceu em 10 de janeiro de 1240, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Edward I King of England em 18 de outubro de 1254, em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 11 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de novembro de 1290, em Harby, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor of Castile Queen of England родилась 10 января 1240 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Fernando III Rey de Castilla y de León было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Queen Consort Jeanne "Juana" De Danmartin Reina Consorte de Castilla, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward I King of England 18 октября 1254 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 11 дочерей. Она умерла 28 ноября 1290 г. в местности харби, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.
